item.title: Meta Launches New Facebook Marketplace App Called Seller
item.author: Eli Tan
item.content:encoded: Seller is part of the Silicon Valley company’s strategy to innovate on its original social network.

item.title: OpenAI Models Go Rogue + Kimi K3 Freakout + A.I. Superforecasting
item.author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Davis Land, John Woo, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano and Leah Shaw Dameron
item.content:encoded: “The story that we’re talking about in this segment was science fiction until Tuesday, OK?”

item.title: AI Minister Jobs: Israel, UK Name Officials to Compete With US, China in AI Race
item.author: Marissa Newman
item.content:encoded: These AI officials are at the mercy of technical breakthroughs from abroad, politics and the onslaught of AI disruption

item.title: An A.I. Music F.A.Q.: Can I Remix Madonna? Is This All Legal?
item.author: Ben Sisario
item.content:encoded: Advances in A.I. are making it possible to create all kinds of music from scratch, but they also raise questions about what is legal and who will actually listen.

item.title: EU Escalates TikTok Probe Over Lack of Teen Privacy Protections
item.author: Gian Volpicelli
item.content:encoded: The European Union escalated a probe against TikTok under its content moderation rulebook, accusing the social network of failing to protect the safety and privacy of teen users.

item.title: SoftBank Weighs Deal for Robotics Startup Gravis
item.author: Min Jeong Lee and Isabel Demetz
item.content:encoded: SoftBank Group Corp. is considering an acquisition of Gravis Robotics AG, people familiar with the matter said, as the Japanese investor targets the artificial intelligence technologies underpinning robotics.

item.title: Bain and LY Are Said to Mull Higher Bid for Kakaku, Rivaling EQT
item.author: Dong Cao, Taro Fuse and Manuel Baigorri
item.content:encoded: Bain Capital and LY Corp. are considering making a fresh joint offer for Japanese online marketplace Kakaku.com Inc., people familiar with the matter said, marking an escalation in the bidding war with private equity firm EQT AB.

item.title: Vietnam Weighs Limiting Social Media Use for Children Under 16
item.author: Cao Ban
item.content:encoded: Vietnam may ban children under 16 years old from posting, commenting or reacting to posts on social media as the country looks to join other governments tightening online protections for minors.

item.title: Tech Stocks Face Investor Skepticism as Rally Stalls and Profit Estimates Rise
item.author: Michael Msika and Henry Ren
item.content:encoded: The earnings season is confirming worries that the technology rally had gone too far, with investors now harder to please and reluctant to buy into rising profit estimates.

item.title: SK Hynix, Samsung and Kioxia Face Pivotal Earnings Test on AI Swings
item.author: Rachel Yeo, Myungshin Cho and Gareth Allan
item.content:encoded: SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. are set to test investor appetite for memory chips as South Korea’s stock market — now a bellwether for global AI sentiment — grapples with violent swings driven by leveraged chip bets.

item.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
item.author: David McCabe
item.content:encoded: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

item.title: Top Environmental Fund Sees Japan Key to AI Energy Challenge
item.author: Aya Wagatsuma
item.content:encoded: Asia’s best-performing environmental fund has increased exposure to Japan as it expects the nation’s technology sector to prove increasingly pivotal in solving the artificial intelligence industry’s surging power demands.

item.title: Robotics Startup Genesis in Talks to Raise at $3 Billion Valuation
item.author: Edward Ludlow, Natasha Mascarenhas, Yazhou Sun and Rebecca Torrence
item.content:encoded: Robotics startup Genesis AI is in talks with investors to raise around $500 million in a new funding round, according to people familiar with the efforts.

item.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
item.author: Don Clark
item.content:encoded: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

item.title: Nvidia Signs $1.5 Billion Accord With Amkor for Chip Packaging
item.author: Nick Turner and Ian King
item.content:encoded: Nvidia Corp. signed a $1.5 billion agreement with Amkor Technology Inc. to help bolster the company’s chip packaging facilities, part of a broader push to expand semiconductor operations in the US.

item.title: SK Hynix Tycoon Ordered to Pay $643 Million Divorce Settlement
item.author: Jaehyun Eom
item.content:encoded: SK Hynix Inc. billionaire Chey Tae-won was ordered by a South Korean court to pay his former wife 944 billion won ($643 million) in a divorce settlement that brings the long-running feud near a conclusion.

item.title: Tech Bonds Hit by Selloff as AI Debt Fears Race Through Markets
item.author: Caleb Mutua and Katherine Schwartz
item.content:encoded: The bonds of some of the biggest US tech companies slid Thursday amid renewed worries about the scale of the debt-fueled artificial-intelligence boom and escalating conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: SAP Cloud Growth Beats Estimates as Customer Orders Speed Up
item.author: Christina Kyriasoglou
item.content:encoded: SAP SE reported better-than-expected sales for its cloud products after pushing enterprise customers to upgrade systems at a faster pace and adopt a suite of artificial intelligence products.

item.title: Intel’s AI-Fueled Forecast Shows Comeback Is Gaining Steam
item.author: Ian King
item.content:encoded: Intel Corp. delivered a revenue forecast that shattered Wall Street estimates as booming data center spending fuels a long-awaited turnaround.

item.title: Alphabet’s Anthropic Stake Jumps to Around $124 Billion
item.author: Davey Alba
item.content:encoded: The value of Alphabet Inc.’s stake in the artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC has soared to around $124 billion, making it one of the most lucrative bets in the company’s history.

item.title: Trump Expands AI Data Center Pledge in Bid to Ease Power Costs
item.author: Jennifer A. Dlouhy and Jeff Mason
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump on Thursday welcomed fresh commitments from power utilities and data center developers to put technology companies on the hook to pay for the electricity they need to drive energy-hungry artificial-intelligence systems.

item.title: US to Produce Ukraine’s Magura Sea Drones for the First Time
item.author: Sonya Dymova
item.content:encoded: A US manufacturer is set to produce Ukraine-designed, battle-tested boat drones for the first time, as the White House bids to bolster America’s military production and expand its unmanned arsenal.

item.title: Google Hit With $1 Billion Fine By EU Over Search Engine Practices
item.author: Adam Satariano and Jeanna Smialek
item.content:encoded: At a tense moment for trans-Atlantic trade, the European Union accused Google of anti-competitive business practices.

item.title: China Rewrites the ‘Soft Power’ Playbook for the A.I. Age
item.author: Steve Lohr
item.content:encoded: China is seeking global influence and good will with its open, low-cost artificial intelligence software.

item.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
item.author: Ivan Penn
item.content:encoded: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
item.author: Eli Tan and Cecilia Kang
item.content:encoded: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found liable in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

item.title: Ford Will Use Apple Maps in New Self-Driving System
item.author: Jack Ewing
item.content:encoded: In a first, Apple software will influence the way a new line of electric vehicles made by Ford Motor operates, and other automakers may follow.

item.title: Tesla Profit Falls Even as Car Sales Rebound
item.author: Jack Ewing
item.content:encoded: The company is selling more cars, but the company’s profit was down because of price cuts and higher expenses.

item.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
item.author: Brian X. Chen
item.content:encoded: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

item.title: Infosys Taps Veteran to Steer Software Leader Through AI Turmoil
item.author: Sankalp Phartiyal
item.content:encoded: Infosys Ltd. named Ashiss Kumar Dash chief executive officer, tapping a company veteran with US experience to revive sales growth at an IT and software giant challenged by the rise of AI.

item.title: SpaceX Delays Major Flight Test Again in Post-IPO Spotlight
item.author: Sana Pashankar
item.content:encoded: SpaceX delayed to Friday the next major test of its massive Starship rocket after also abandoning takeoff last week, heightening the stakes for the critical milestone.

item.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
item.author: David Yaffe-Bellany
item.content:encoded: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

item.title: Amazon Investigated Over Chinese Influence by Senate Panel
item.author: Spencer Soper
item.content:encoded: Amazon.com Inc. is being investigated by the Senate Small Business Committee for allegedly letting China influence the company’s online marketplace, according to a document reviewed by Bloomberg and two people interviewed as part of the investigation.

item.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
item.author: Kate Conger
item.content:encoded: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

item.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
item.author: Stuart A. Thompson and Tiffany Hsu
item.content:encoded: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

item.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
item.author: Kate Conger
item.content:encoded: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

item.title: What Happened When Meta Used A.I. to Ban Accounts on Facebook and Instagram
item.author: Stuart A. Thompson and Eli Tan
item.content:encoded: Users said the technology mistakenly deleted their accounts. To resolve the issue, they still had to rely on A.I.

item.title: Google Releases Three New Gemini A.I. Models
item.author: Kate Conger
item.content:encoded: The models include one that is the company’s most powerful and another that is fine-tuned for cybersecurity, as Google competes with rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: When Listening to Music Was a Team Sport
item.author: Walker Mimms
item.content:encoded: An exhibition at the Cooper Hewitt celebrates the glorious machines that gave us the soundtracks of our lives before streaming sites shoved them toward obsolescence.

item.title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
item.author: Kate Conger
item.content:encoded: As Google incorporates more artificial intelligence into search, people are spending more time on Google. Some website operators are crying foul.

item.title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
item.author: David McCabe
item.content:encoded: The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.

item.title: A.I. ‘Vibecoded’ Apps Are Flooding Apple’s App Store
item.author: Kalley Huang
item.content:encoded: Mobile apps are easy to make with artificial intelligence. That doesn’t mean people are excited to use them.

item.title: Why Silicon Valley Can’t Stop Looking Over Its Shoulder at China
item.author: Cade Metz
item.content:encoded: Chinese companies are offering artificial intelligence that is nearly as good as the leading U.S. technologies, but it’s still America’s race to lose.

item.title: The ‘Bad Blood’ Between Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi’s Tarek Mansour
item.author: David Yaffe-Bellany and William K. Rashbaum
item.content:encoded: Shayne Coplan of Polymarket and Tarek Mansour of Kalshi are young billionaires intent on dominating prediction markets. It’s more than a battle. It’s “bad blood.”

item.title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live
item.author: Victor Mather
item.content:encoded: Everyone is watching the goals after they happen, and some get them significantly faster.

item.title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
item.author: Michael S. Rosenwald
item.content:encoded: A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.

item.title: Meta in Talks to Lease Computing Power to Anthropic in Potential $10 Billion Deal
item.author: Eli Tan and Mike Isaac
item.content:encoded: A deal would underline how scarce computing power is for artificial intelligence development, and could create a new business for Meta.
